hero.title: We design lending platforms AI-first. From diagnostic to prototype in weeks.
hero.tagline: Fit for Digital. Fit for AI.
hero.body: We rebuild the core that leasing, consumer finance, and fleet operators have outgrown. Diagnostic in weeks. Configured Asset Finance or Fleet prototypes on your data within a quarter; Working Capital, Consumer Credit, and Secured Microcredit follow a longer engagement path. Nearly two decades of European leasing operations, Central and Eastern Europe included, behind every decision.

problem.title: Mid-tier operators are running on stacks they have outgrown.
problem.body: Leasing companies, captives, non-bank lenders, and fleet operators reach a point where the existing systems cannot keep up with new products, new channels, and new compliance demands. By the time the rebuild board paper gets approved, the next product cycle is already lost and the AI-native challengers have shipped twice. Innovia closes the gap as a specialist advisory practice with a working platform behind it, not a slide pack and a partner search.

thesis.title: Three properties hold value as the UI layer commoditises.
thesis.intro: LLMs and workflow orchestration are eating the front end of every enterprise application. What stays defensible is structural, not cosmetic. We built Innovia around these three from the first commit.

isolation.title: Verified isolation
isolation.body: Twenty-five integration tests in CI prove tenant boundaries on every build. Not a promise in the contract, not a diagram in the architecture deck.

codebase.title: Single codebase
codebase.body: One stack that compounds across audit, AI, schema, and deployment. A portal that unifies five backends still leaves five backends.

aiNative.title: AI-native from day one
aiNative.body: Anthropic vision over documents, MCP-callable tools, agent-grade audit log. Built in from the first commit, not bolted on after a board memo.

services.title: Diagnostics, redesign, prototype, implementation.
services.body: Innovia operates as a specialist consultancy with technology delivery built in. The four engagement types stack: most partners start with a diagnostic and move through redesign into prototype deployment and implementation support.

diagnostics.title: Diagnostics
diagnostics.body: A structured read on where your origination, decisioning, contract lifecycle, and reporting stack stand against current operator benchmarks. Decision-grade output the leadership team can act on.

redesign.title: Operating-model redesign
redesign.body: Target operating model for the segments you serve: org, process, data, technology. Sequenced to what the business can actually absorb.

prototype.title: Software prototyping
prototype.body: Working prototypes on the Innovia platform, configured to your data model and products. Ready for live testing within weeks.

implementation.title: Implementation support
implementation.body: From prototype to production: integration, data migration, change management, hypercare. We stay until the platform runs the business, not the other way around.

modules.title: Five verticals, one engine.
modules.body: Prototyping and implementation engagements run on the Innovia platform. Five modules share the same engine, data model, and workflow orchestrator. Asset Finance and Fleet are production-quality engineering, awaiting first design-partner go-live. Working Capital, Consumer Credit, and Secured Microcredit are pre-production for H2 2026.

assetFinance.title: Asset Finance
assetFinance.body: Origination, scoring, contract lifecycle, billing, accounting. End-to-end for financial and operational lease, with cross-tenant group reporting.

fleet.title: Fleet
fleet.body: Vehicle and service management for captives and independent fleets. Telematics-ready with live adapters configurable per customer, cost-per-km and residual value analytics.

workingCapital.title: Working Capital
workingCapital.body: Corporate credit lines, drawdowns, interest runs, covenant tracking. Revolving, term, and uncommitted facilities, distinct from dealer floorplan.

consumerCredit.title: Consumer Credit
consumerCredit.body: Point-of-sale and direct-to-consumer lending. Decisioning built for minutes, merchant onboarding and KYC + AML workflow built in (bureau and screening providers wired per engagement).

securedMicrocredit.title: Secured Microcredit
securedMicrocredit.body: Pawn-style, partner-channel, and merchant-bundled lending. Collateral tracking, rapid disbursement, full-cycle compliance.

useCases.title: Just a few of the workflows the platform runs. Plenty more behind them.
useCases.body: Screens captured from the live Innovia application, soft-blurred so the structure stays legible but specific data remains private. Each card maps to a real role and a real workflow already operating against demo data.
useCases.note: Captured from Innovia trunk. Blurred to protect demo data. Sharp walkthroughs available on request.

executiveDashboard.title: Portfolio command centre
executiveDashboard.body: Admins land on a single screen that consolidates originations, the active book, arrears, and tenant-level KPIs across every brand and country. Each tile drills straight into the underlying contracts, partners, or accounting entries without leaving the session. Group COOs get a side-by-side view of subsidiaries that used to take a weekly Excel reconciliation.
executiveDashboard.usp: One pane of glass for the whole group, with full drill-down to the source entry.

dealPipeline.title: Deal pipeline
dealPipeline.body: Sales operators move a deal from inquiry through analysis to a signed contract in one workspace, without re-keying anything between stages. Pipeline value, time-to-yes, approval rate, and stage breakdowns track on top of every product variant. Direct, dealer-introduced, and broker channels share the same funnel, so commercial teams compare apples to apples.
dealPipeline.usp: One funnel for every channel, with cycle time visible from the first click.

financialScoring.title: Financial scoring engine
financialScoring.body: Five-dimension corporate scoring (revenue, EBITDA margin, leverage, liquidity, equity ratio) runs against the deal record and stores the score, the inputs, and the model version that produced it. Underwriters get a defensible rationale instead of a black-box answer, and every decision stays auditable months later when the risk committee or the regulator asks.
financialScoring.usp: Explainable scores kept on the deal forever, so 'why did we approve this?' has a one-click answer.

contractManagement.title: Contract management
contractManagement.body: The full leasing book lives in one searchable list with status, financed value, average interest, and active contract counts in view at all times. Drilling into a contract opens the schedule, the related services, the documents, and the audit trail in the same workspace. Servicing teams stop swivel-chairing between three systems to answer a single client question.
contractManagement.usp: Every contract, every document, one workspace, with no swivel-chair between systems.

collections.title: Collections and dunning
collections.body: Aging buckets, overdue per contract, risk-flagged accounts, and a daily action queue point collectors at the right cases first thing in the morning. A single click moves from a missed payment to the dunning workflow, the client history, and the contact log. Recoveries shift from spreadsheet triage to a prioritised work list driven by exposure and probability of cure.
collections.usp: Collectors start the day on a prioritised queue, not a 9 AM spreadsheet.

productCatalog.title: Financial product catalogue
productCatalog.body: Operating Lease, Finance Lease, Hire Purchase, corporate loans, EV and green variants all sit in one catalogue with currency, schedule type, interest, and advance bands configured per product. New products go live in hours instead of release cycles because parameters are data, not code. Country teams can launch a market-specific variant without waiting for the next sprint.
productCatalog.usp: New products in hours, not releases, because the catalogue is data.

insuranceCatalog.title: Insurance products
insuranceCatalog.body: CASCO, GAP, life, and asset-specific insurance products are wired directly to the financed amount and to broker margin economics. Activation per market and pricing per asset class are configuration, not a code change. Attach rate becomes a lever the commercial team controls, and the impact on yield shows up in the same dashboard as the underlying contracts.
insuranceCatalog.usp: Insurance attach is a configuration lever, not a quarterly project.

fleetOperations.title: Client fleet view
fleetOperations.body: Fleet managers see the whole estate in one frame: size, financed value, residual exposure, contracts expiring, suspended assets, and a renewal timeline that highlights what needs attention in the next ninety days. Renewals stop being a fire drill in month thirty-five, and corporate clients gain a self-service surface that replaces the monthly status call with the back office.
fleetOperations.usp: Renewals planned ninety days out, not chased in the last week of month thirty-five.

dealerPortal.title: Dealer portal
dealerPortal.body: Dealers quote, apply, and track financing from their own portal, with active campaigns surfacing the subvented rate, RV uplift, and remaining-volume terms in line with the offer. Time-to-yes for a dealer-introduced deal drops from days to minutes because the dealer captures clean data once. Subvention stays under captive control, and the friction at the point of sale goes away.
dealerPortal.usp: Showroom-speed financing, with subvention economics still owned by the captive.

clientPortal.title: Client portal
clientPortal.body: Retail and corporate borrowers see their schedule, paid versus remaining, upcoming payments, overdue alerts, and active services without ever opening a ticket. Self-service deflects the routine call volume that fills back-office queues, so agents focus on cases that actually need a human. NPS goes up, cost-to-serve goes down, and the borrower stays in control of their own contract.
clientPortal.usp: Borrowers in control, back office freed for cases that actually need a human.

glPosting.title: GL posting and export
glPosting.body: Every contract event (origination, billing, payment, write-off) posts to the general ledger through a deterministic mapping configured per product and per country. Export batches reconcile against the back-end ERP without a parallel finance spreadsheet, and any entry can be traced back to the contract event that produced it. Finance teams close the month from the same workspace they use to investigate exceptions.
glPosting.usp: Every contract event posts to GL with an auditable mapping, no parallel finance spreadsheet.

corporateCreditLines.title: Corporate credit lines
corporateCreditLines.body: Revolving facilities, term loans, and uncommitted lines live alongside the leasing book, with drawdowns, repayments, interest accruals, and covenant checks running on the same engine. A relationship manager sees exposure across leasing and lending in one view rather than reconciling between systems. Corporate banking workflows ship native, so a platform that started as a leasing core can carry the full client wallet.
corporateCreditLines.usp: Corporate banking workflows native to the same platform that runs your leasing book.

workflowOrchestration.title: Workflow orchestration
workflowOrchestration.body: Business processes (origination, KYC, contract activation, document collection, dunning escalation) run as versioned workflows on an embedded orchestrator with full observability, retries, and human-in-the-loop tasks. Changing a step is a workflow edit, not a sprint, and every execution is auditable. Operations and product teams stop arguing with engineers about which path the system actually takes.
workflowOrchestration.usp: Business processes as versioned, observable workflows, not buried in code.

kycOnboarding.title: Client onboarding and beneficial owners
kycOnboarding.body: Corporate clients are modeled with the full beneficial-owner chain: directors, shareholders, and ultimate beneficial owners across nested ownership structures. Onboarding captures identity, residency, PEP and sanctions flags in one workflow, and the resulting graph travels with every deal for the life of the relationship. No more flat customer records that hide who actually owns the cash flow.
kycOnboarding.usp: Full beneficial-owner chain modeled at onboarding, not a flat customer record.

modelMonitoring.title: ML risk advisory and model monitoring
modelMonitoring.body: Risk scoring is augmented by an explainable ML layer that runs alongside the rules engine, with feature importance, calibration, and drift tracked on a dedicated monitoring page. Underwriters see model agreement and disagreement on every deal, and risk leadership sees when a model needs retraining before performance degrades. The augmentation is advisory, never autonomous, so the human signs off.
modelMonitoring.usp: Scoring augmented by explainable ML, with drift and calibration monitored in plain sight.

partnership.title: Innovation Partnership.
partnership.body: Year one runs as a co-creation engagement. The partner gets a configured deployment, decision rights on their module roadmap, and the operator depth that comes from nearly two decades of running European leasing platforms.

configured.title: Configured deployment
configured.body: A working platform shaped to the partner's data model, products, and processes from day one.

decision.title: Decision rights
decision.body: The partner co-owns the module roadmap. What ships first, second, and third reflects their priorities.

operator.title: Operator depth
operator.body: Nearly two decades operating European leasing platforms. Engagements start from operator-grade domain context.

outcome.title: Concrete outcome
outcome.body: At the end of Year 1 you have a deployment running your products on your data, not a stack of slides and a vendor selection memo.

operator.title: Built by an operator.
operator.body: Nearly two decades operating European leasing platforms across automotive, equipment, and captive finance, in mid-tier and group settings. The work covered migrations, multi-country rollouts, credit-policy redesign, regulatory change, and the unglamorous month-end firefighting that turns a platform from theoretical to trusted. The product roadmap and the advisory practice both reflect what actually breaks in production.

hero.title: A four-week path to your next board-grade decision.
hero.tagline: Fit for Digital. Fit for AI.
hero.bodyPara1: A productized advisory engagement, sold at fixed price and fixed scope, that delivers a single outcome: a sponsor who can make a defensible, board-grade decision they could not have made before the sprint.
hero.bodyPara2: What makes it different is the combination. Top-tier consulting discipline (MECE structure, sourced numbers, pyramid principle, no rhetorical claims) on every deliverable you receive. Working software you log into in week two, configured in your colors with a sample of your data. Fixed price, fixed scope, fixed four-week calendar, no scope creep dressed up as discovery. Led by an operator with nearly two decades shipping, supporting, and scaling European leasing platforms.
hero.bodyPara3: Twelve to fifteen deliverables you keep, in English, board-ready. One decision moment at the readout, three states only: yes, no, or a named deadline. The Innovation Partnership is pre-teased throughout, ready if you decide to proceed.

product.title: Four weeks. Fixed price. One decision the board can defend.

operator.title: Operator depth, not theory.
operator.body: Led by a founder with nearly two decades operating European leasing platforms. Every recommendation is informed by having shipped, supported, and scaled the systems we discuss.

software.title: Working software, not just slides.
software.body: By week two you log into a working platform configured in your colors, with your products and a sample of your data. The readout walks through it live, not from screenshots.

fixed.title: Fixed price, fixed scope, fixed time.
fixed.body: A productized engagement, not an open-ended consultancy. You know exactly what you pay, what you get, and when you get it. Scope is locked in the Statement of Work. Change orders are billed transparently at a day rate.

intake.label: Intake
intake.body: Statement of Work and mutual NDA signed. Sponsor and alternate sponsor confirmed. Scope locked. Five to eight stakeholder interview slots booked. Data drop confirmed.

discovery.label: Discovery
discovery.body: Five to eight interviews, capability assessment, process maps, gap report with sourced numbers. Mid-sprint synchronization with the sponsor at the end of the week.

design.label: Design
design.body: Target operating model, future-state journeys, capability roadmap prioritized in waves. Branded working tenant configured with your products and a sample of your data.

decide.label: Decide
decide.body: Executive memo, board deck, business case with sensitivity on the three variables that matter most. Decision pre-read with the sponsor three days before the readout.

readout.label: Readout
readout.body: Two hours on-site. Live walkthrough on the branded tenant. Innovation Partnership proposal presented. Explicit decision moment: yes, no, or named deadline.

memo.title: Executive memo
memo.body: One to two pages. Situation, complication, recommendation, ask. Designed for a chief executive to read on a phone.

deck.title: Board deck
deck.body: Fifteen to twenty-five slides, every title a takeaway. Used as the basis for your next board paper.

businessCase.title: Business case
businessCase.body: Narrative plus spreadsheet. NPV, payback, sensitivity on the top three variables, risk-adjusted scenarios.

tom.title: Target operating model
tom.body: People, process, technology, data, governance. Decision rights named, transition outline included.

roadmap.title: Capability roadmap
roadmap.body: Twelve to thirty-six months. Each initiative with effort, impact, dependencies, and owner.

tenant.title: Branded working tenant
tenant.body: A configured instance of the Innovia platform in your colors, with your products, that you can log into during the readout and keep exploring for 90 days after.

tiers.title: Three tiers. Standard is the default.
tiers.footnote: Pricing is locked at scoping, never adjusted mid-sprint. Change orders are billed transparently at €2,000 per day. Payment terms: 50% on signature, 50% on readout. Travel within the EU included.

lite.title: Lite
lite.body: Two weeks. Diagnostic and executive memo only. For prospects who need a fast read before committing to a full sprint.

standard.title: Standard
standard.body: Three to four weeks. The full diagnostic, design, and decision pack. The default tier for most engagements.

premium.title: Premium
premium.body: Six weeks. Standard plus deeper process and integration work. For engagements that anticipate immediate execution.

who.a: Chief operating, information, or risk officers at independent financial institutions, leasing companies, fleet operators, consumer finance and corporate credit firms across Europe. The sweet spot is a mid-tier operator facing the same questions as the Tier 1 banks, with less runway to answer them.

outcome.q: What if the decision is no?
outcome.a: A clear no is a perfectly valid outcome of the engagement, and the deliverables are still yours. The sprint is paid for execution and a defensible decision pack, not for a specific direction. We treat a clear no with the same respect as a clear yes.

scope.q: What if we need more or less than four weeks?
scope.a: Lite is two weeks for prospects who need a fast diagnostic. Standard is three to four weeks and covers most engagements. Premium is six weeks when immediate execution is expected. Scope is fixed at signature and can only be expanded via written change orders at €2,000 per day.

data.a: Every engagement is bound by a mutual non-disclosure agreement signed before the sprint begins. We work with anonymized data samples; we do not require access to personal data or live systems. Interview recordings are retained for thirty days after engagement close, then deleted.

after.a: If the decision is yes, the Innovation Partnership scoping starts within five business days. The Partnership is a longer engagement that takes the recommendation into execution, sized between €60k and €150k depending on scope. If the decision is no or a named deadline, the engagement closes cleanly with no further outreach unless you reinitiate.

cta.title: Let us see if a sprint is the right next move.
cta.body: A thirty-minute discovery call is free, with no obligation. We confirm whether a Fit Sprint fits your situation and your decision timeline. If it does not, we say so directly.

diagnostic.title: Fit for Digital. Fit for AI.
diagnostic.subtitle: A 7-minute read on where your operation actually sits.
diagnostic.intro1: Twenty questions across seven dimensions: core platform, configuration and speed, data and decisioning, compliance and audit, AI readiness, customer experience, and employee experience. The bar is set at operator-grade, not enterprise-baseline.
diagnostic.intro2: No email, no signup. The result lives in your browser and your browser only. If the score looks honest, the next step is a conversation.

result.ctaTitle: The score is the easy part. The conversation is where it gets useful.
result.ctaBody: If anything in this breakdown surprised you, that is exactly the kind of thing the first 45 minutes are good for. Your roadmap, our prototype, an honest read on fit.

exposed.title: Significant exposure.
exposed.body: Several of the seven dimensions are at or below the threshold where regulatory, competitive, or operational risk compounds quickly. The next platform decision should not be a procurement exercise.

gaps.title: Foundational gaps.
gaps.body: This is where most mid-tier operators in lending sit on an honest first read. The base is functional, but core, configuration, and AI readiness are not yet at operator-grade. The good news is that the gap is closable in a single Innovation Partnership cycle.

strong.title: Strong base, AI gap.
strong.body: The core and the operational layers are in good shape. The opportunity is in moving from configured to truly fluid, and in turning the AI readiness score from a gap into a moat.

operator.title: Operator-grade.
operator.body: Few operators land here. If this is genuinely your state, the conversation is less about platform and more about how to keep the lead. Partnership models that exchange operator depth go in both directions.

whitepapersIndex.title: The Innovia view.
whitepapersIndex.body: Opinionated, operator-grade notes on where the lending stack is moving. New papers as the platform expands.

changelog.title: What ships on the platform.
changelog.subtitle: Dated entries of capability changes that matter to a buyer. Internal refactors and minor fixes stay out of this page.
changelog.cadence: We update on the day the change is in production. No quarterly summary, no surprises.
changelog.whitepapersFooter: Why these capabilities, in the order they ship, follow our positioning thesis:

contact.title: Sizing a platform decision for 2026-2027?
contact.body: Open to a conversation if you operate in any of the five segments above. The first call is a 45-minute working session: your roadmap, our prototype, an honest read on fit.

footer.tagline: Fit for Digital. Fit for AI.
